items.q: What is the capacity of short-term memory according to Miller?
items.why: Students confuse capacity (items) with duration (about 18-30 seconds without rehearsal). Miller's magic number describes how many CHUNKS, not how long.

items.q: According to Maslow, when can self-actualisation be achieved?
items.correct: Only after lower deficiency needs are largely met.
items.misconception: Anyone can self-actualise at any time through willpower.
items.why: Students miss the hierarchical structure. You cannot pursue growth needs while physiological, safety, love, and esteem needs are unmet - that's the whole point of a hierarchy.

items.q: What does the dopamine hypothesis of schizophrenia state?
items.correct: Positive symptoms are linked to excess dopamine activity in mesolimbic pathways.
items.misconception: Schizophrenia is caused by too little dopamine.
items.random_wrong: Schizophrenia is caused by too much serotonin.
items.why: Students reverse the direction. The original hypothesis was HYPERdopaminergia. The revised version notes hypoactivity in prefrontal areas for negative symptoms, but excess in mesolimbic for positive.

items.q: Top-down offender profiling was developed by:
items.why: Correct answer: FBI. “Canter” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Differential association theory was proposed by:
items.why: It's Sutherland. “Eysenck” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year did Lombroso publish his atavistic form theory?
items.why: Exact answer: 1876 AD. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
